summary: axiosqqq: axios typosquat smuggling an undeclared 'math' dependency
details: axiosqqq is a near-identical clone of the popular axios HTTP client. The package name is a suffix-edit typosquat of axios, the bundled dist/node/axios.cjs carries the 'Axios v1.16.1' header, the repository field still points at github.com/axios/axios, and the public API surface (axios, AxiosError, CancelToken, etc.) is preserved so a developer who mistypes the install command receives a package that behaves like real axios. package.json declares an additional runtime dependency 'math': '^0.0.3' that does not appear in legitimate axios and is never required or imported anywhere in lib/ or dist/. Installing axiosqqq nevertheless causes npm to resolve and install 'math@^0.0.3' into the installer's tree, where any lifecycle scripts or import-time code in that third-party package will execute. The combination of an axios-faithful clone, a confusion-style name, and an unused extra dependency is the canonical 'smuggle a payload via a typosquat lure' shape: the lure is benign-looking, and the harm rides in via the silently pulled transitive.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
